feature: Navigation refactoring - NavigationBar.jsx extraction and AuthContext.jsx separation
summary: Successfully verified the navigation refactoring. NavigationBar.jsx was correctly extracted from App.js and AuthContext.jsx now provides auth utilities independently. All navigation features work correctly for both public and authenticated users.

public_navigation.name: Public navbar renders for unauthenticated users

login_flow.name: Redirect to home after login
login_flow.details: User redirected to / after successful login

authenticated_navigation.name: Admin dropdown
authenticated_navigation.details: 8 menu items visible - Domain Config, User Management, Content Moderation, Analytics, Notifications, DDEX Admin, Sponsorship Admin, Industry Admin

navigation_routing.name: Profile Settings page
navigation_routing.details: /profile/settings loads with ProfileSettings component using useAuth from contexts/AuthContext.jsx

logout_flow.name: Logout functionality
logout_flow.details: User logged out, redirected to home, public navbar shown

logout_flow.name: Protected route redirect
logout_flow.details: Accessing /profile/settings when logged out redirects to /login

admin_user_test.name: Admin dropdown visible for admin users
admin_user_test.details: Red Admin button with gear icon visible, 8 admin links present

context_for_next_testing_agent: Navigation refactoring verified successfully. NavigationBar.jsx extracted from App.js into /components/layout/NavigationBar.jsx. AuthContext.jsx created at /contexts/AuthContext.jsx with useAuth, AuthProvider, ProtectedRoute, AdminRoute exports. App.js re-exports useAuth for backward compatibility. ProfileSettings.js updated to import useAuth from contexts/AuthContext.

features: Admin dropdown visible only for admin users (uses isAdmin() from useAuth)
ProtectedRoute redirects to /login if not authenticated

added_imports: import { useAuth, AuthProvider, ProtectedRoute, AdminRoute } from './contexts/AuthContext'
App.js_changes.re_export: export { useAuth } from './contexts/AuthContext' for backward compatibility
